channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge sides with Trump: anyone in US illegally must register with fed government
item.description: A federal judge ruled on Thursday that anyone in the U.S. illegally must register with the federal government by providing their fingerprints and address, and carry documentation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink to step down amid high-stakes war diplomacy
item.description: Biden-appointed Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink is preparing to step down from her post less than three years on the job at a pivotal moment in the Ukraine-Russia war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must work to bring back mistakenly deported Maryland man
item.description: The Supreme Court says the Trump administration must work to bring back a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: States advance fetal rights measures that critics warn will pave a path for outlawing abortions
item.description: A new Kansas law and a bill advancing in Florida outline policies backed by abortion opponents that critics see as moves toward giving embryos and fetuses the same rights as the women carrying them.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And John Hanna, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge allows requirement that everyone in the US illegally must register to move forward
item.description: A federal judge is allowing the Trump administration to move forward with a requirement that everyone in the U.S. illegally must register with the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Red states get in line with Trump's DEI ban in schools as compliance deadline nears
item.description: Trump's Department of Education gave public schools, universities until April 24 to certify compliance with his federal mandate to strip all DEI policies or face federal funding cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House approves budget framework for Trump's 'big' bill after intense wrangling sways GOP holdouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans narrowly approved their budget framework Thursday, a political turnaround after Speaker Mike Johnson worked into the night to satisfy GOP holdouts who had refused to advance trillions of dollars in tax breaks without deeper spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US budget deficit grows to $1.3 trillion, the second highest six-month level on record
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. budget deficit has grown to more than $1.3 trillion in the first half of the 2025 fiscal year — the second highest six-month deficit on record, according to Treasury Department data released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pronouns in some reporters' email signatures get a stony response from Trump administration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — You know those email signatures at the end of messages? The ones that include a range of information about the senders — phone numbers, addresses, social media handles.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawley demands Zuckerberg testify on alleged ties between Meta, China
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley is calling on Mark Zuckerberg to testify after a Facebook whistleblower alleged in a Senate hearing that the company had ties to the Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard says RFK, MLK Jr records ‘ready to release’ in days, has 'hunters' looking at FBI, CIA for more files
item.description: The Trump administration will release files on 1968 assassinations of Robert F. Kennedy and Martin Luther King Jr., Tulsi Gabbard said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department seeks death penalty for man who killed fellow supermax inmate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department will seek the death penalty for a man accused of killing a fellow prisoner at the federal government’s “Supermax” prison in Florence, Colorado, while serving a life sentence for killing his cellmate at another lockup, the department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Dept upgrades travel advisory for El Salvador, considered safer than France, other European countries
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio credited Nayib Bukele's leadership as the U.S. travel advisory for El Salvador was upgraded to the safest designation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tim Walz heckled by veterans for stolen valor claims, China connections
item.description: Veterans at the Minnesota State Capitol called Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz a "coward" and criticized him for sympathizing with China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff spike fuels new House bill to lock China out of US government tech
item.description: A bipartisan duo in the House is leading a crackdown on Chinese technology in the U.S. after President Donald Trump's tariff announcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Another U-Turn: Trump reverses tariffs that caused market meltdown, but companies remain bewildered
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump delivered another jarring reversal in American trade policy Wednesday, suspending for 90 days import taxes he’d imposed barely 13 hours earlier on dozens of countries while escalating his trade war with China.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump tariff drama
item.description: Financial markets soared on Wednesday after President Trump issued a 90-day pause in almost all the tariffs that had roiled the global economy during the past week.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Billionaires, trusted allies, media personalities. These are the people Trump picked for top roles
item.description: President Donald Trump prioritized loyalty as he built out a team for his second term, surrounding himself with people who served faithfully in his first administration or who worked on one or more of his three campaigns.
item.dc:creator: PARKER KAUFMANN, HUMERA LODHI and CHRISTOPHER KELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. stocks soar after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks surged on Wednesday after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on the higher tariffs he had imposed on most countries — except for China.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam, MPR News

item.title: Ozempic and Wegovy: Sen. Fetterman pushes for Medicare to cover GLP-1s
item.description: Fetterman appealed to President Trump and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. personally.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA to revoke $188M in New York City migrant grants
item.description: The money is part of the Shelter and Services Program, which helps local governments provide for migrants.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: US trade representative testifies before House on Trump trade agenda
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer testified before the House Ways and Means Committee on Wednesday morning on President Trump's trade agenda, as the latest reciprocal tariffs took effect overnight.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks soar after Trump announces 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for some
item.description: Although markets opened mixed on Wednesday, stocks soared after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Companies need 'time machine' for tariffs to make sense: Andrew Yang
item.description: "Instead of reshoring manufacturing, [CEOs] are just cutting investment, they're laying people off," Yang said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Biden believes he would've beaten Trump in election: Author
item.description: Author Chris Whipple released "Uncharted: How Trump Beat Biden, Harris, and the Odds in the Wildest Campaign in History" on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Amy Coney Barrett is what is needed in Supreme Court justice: Analyst
item.description: Barrett has sided with liberal justices on multiple hot-button issues, including DOGE regulation and Arizona’s voter ID law.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Weber, NewsNation

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump move forward with firing thousands of federal workers
item.description: The Supreme Court's stay, which allows the administration to execute the firings for now while it litigates in federal court, does not mean the terminations were lawful.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Christina Gatti, MPR News

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
